$comment: Pre-configured built-in demo segments. Seeded on first boot. These are read-only and can be duplicated by users to create custom variants.

segments.title: Login & Dashboard Overview
segments.description: Learn how to log in to the platform and explore the main dashboard, including widgets, quick actions, and navigation.

steps.value: Welcome to Zorbit! Let's start by logging in to the platform.
steps.narration: Welcome to Zorbit. Let's start by logging in to the platform.
First, navigate to the login page.
Enter your email address in this field.

steps.value: demo@zorbit-platform.com

steps.narration: Now enter your password.

steps.value: demo-password

steps.narration: Click the login button to sign in.
The dashboard is loading.

steps.value: You are now on the main dashboard. Here you can see your widgets, quick actions, and platform overview.
steps.narration: You are now on the main dashboard. Here you can see your widgets, quick actions, and platform overview.
Use the sidebar menu to navigate between platform sections.

segments.title: User Management
segments.description: Create, edit, and manage user accounts. Learn about user roles and organization assignments.

steps.value: In this segment, we'll learn how to manage users on the platform.
steps.narration: In this segment, we'll learn how to manage users on the platform.
Navigate to the Users page.
This table shows all users in your organization.
Click here to create a new user.
Let's open the create user form.

steps.value: Fill in the user details: name, email, and role assignment. Sensitive data like email is stored securely in the PII Vault.
steps.narration: Fill in the user details. Sensitive data like email is stored securely in the PII Vault.
Assign one or more roles to control what the user can access.

steps.value: That's it! Users can now log in and access features based on their assigned roles.
steps.narration: That's it. Users can now log in and access features based on their assigned roles.

segments.title: Organization Management
segments.description: Set up and manage organizations, departments, and the org hierarchy tree.

steps.value: Organizations are the top-level namespace in Zorbit. Let's explore how to manage them.
steps.narration: Organizations are the top-level namespace in Zorbit. Let's explore how to manage them.
Navigate to the Organizations page.
This tree view shows your organization hierarchy.
Click here to create a child organization or department.

steps.value: Each organization has its own namespace, users, roles, and data isolation.
steps.narration: Each organization has its own namespace, users, roles, and data isolation.

steps.value: Namespace isolation ensures that data in one organization cannot be accessed by another.
steps.narration: Namespace isolation ensures that data in one organization cannot be accessed by another.

segments.title: Role & Privilege Assignment
segments.description: Create roles, assign privileges, and understand how privilege sections map to menu items.

steps.value: Roles and privileges control what users can see and do on the platform.
steps.narration: Roles and privileges control what users can see and do on the platform.
Navigate to the Roles page.
This table shows all roles defined for your organization.
Click here to create a new role.
Let's create a new role.

steps.value: Give the role a name and description, then assign privileges from the privilege sections.
steps.narration: Give the role a name and description, then assign privileges from the privilege sections.
Let's look at the available privileges.
Privileges are organized into sections. Each section maps to a menu category.

steps.value: When a role is assigned a privilege, the corresponding menu item becomes visible to users with that role.
steps.narration: When a role is assigned a privilege, the corresponding menu item becomes visible to users with that role.

steps.value: This is how Zorbit achieves privilege-driven navigation.
steps.narration: This is how Zorbit achieves privilege-driven navigation.

segments.title: Navigation Configuration
segments.description: Understand how the dynamic menu works, including sections, privileges, and handlebars route templating.

steps.value: Zorbit generates navigation menus dynamically based on user roles and privileges.
steps.narration: Zorbit generates navigation menus dynamically based on user roles and privileges.
Navigate to the Menu Configuration page.
These are the menu sections. Each section groups related privileges.

steps.value: Route templates use handlebars syntax like {{org_id}} which are resolved at runtime to the actual organization ID.
steps.narration: Route templates use handlebars syntax. Variables like org ID are resolved at runtime.

steps.value: The menu can fall back to a static configuration if the database is unavailable.
steps.narration: The menu can fall back to a static configuration if the database is unavailable.

segments.title: Audit Log Review
segments.description: Browse audit logs, filter by time range, and understand the audit event structure.

steps.value: Every write operation on the platform generates an audit log entry.
steps.narration: Every write operation on the platform generates an audit log entry.
Navigate to the Audit Logs page.
This table shows all audit events for your organization.
Use the time range filter to narrow down results.
Filter by event type to find specific actions.

steps.value: Each audit entry includes the actor, action, timestamp, and a correlation ID for distributed tracing.
steps.narration: Each audit entry includes the actor, action, timestamp, and a correlation ID for distributed tracing.

segments.title: PII Vault Operations
segments.description: Learn how sensitive data is tokenized and stored securely in the PII Vault.

steps.value: The PII Vault stores sensitive personal data separately from operational databases. Only tokens are stored in application tables.
steps.narration: The PII Vault stores sensitive personal data separately from operational databases.
Navigate to the Token Explorer.
This table shows PII tokens. The actual sensitive data is encrypted in the vault.

steps.value: When a user is created, their email, phone, and other PII fields are tokenized automatically.
steps.narration: When a user is created, their email and phone are tokenized automatically.

steps.value: Example: email_token PII-92AF maps to the real email stored in the vault.
steps.narration: For example, a PII token maps to the real email stored securely in the vault.
The Vault Status page shows health and statistics.

steps.value: Raw PII must never appear in operational databases, logs, or API responses.
steps.narration: Raw PII must never appear in operational databases, logs, or API responses.

segments.title: Messaging & Events
segments.description: Explore the event-driven messaging system, topics, subscriptions, and the canonical event envelope.

steps.value: Zorbit services communicate asynchronously through Kafka events.
steps.narration: Zorbit services communicate asynchronously through Kafka events.
Navigate to the Event Explorer.
This shows recent events flowing through the platform.

steps.value: Events follow the naming convention: domain.entity.action (e.g. identity.user.created).
steps.narration: Events follow the naming convention domain dot entity dot action.
Let's look at the Kafka topics.
Each service has its own topic for publishing events.
Subscriptions show which services consume which events.

steps.value: Every event uses the canonical event envelope with correlation IDs for distributed tracing.
steps.narration: Every event uses the canonical event envelope with correlation IDs for distributed tracing.

segments.title: Dashboard Designer
segments.description: Create and configure dashboard widgets, set role-based visibility, and arrange the layout.

steps.value: The Dashboard Designer lets you create and arrange widgets for your organization's dashboard.
steps.narration: The Dashboard Designer lets you create and arrange widgets for your organization's dashboard.
Navigate to the Dashboard Designer.
This is the widget grid. You can drag and drop widgets to rearrange them.
Click here to add a new widget.
Let's add a widget.

steps.value: Choose a widget type: Count, Chart, Table, List, or Gauge. Each type has its own configuration options.
steps.narration: Choose a widget type. Each type has its own configuration options.

steps.value: Set the metric query to define where the widget gets its data. Use API path templates with handlebars variables.
steps.narration: Set the metric query to define where the widget gets its data.

steps.value: Assign roles to control which users can see this widget. Leave empty for all users.
steps.narration: Assign roles to control which users can see this widget.

steps.value: Save the layout and switch to view mode to see how the dashboard appears to users.
steps.narration: Save the layout and switch to view mode to see how the dashboard appears to users.

segments.title: Quick Start Onboarding
segments.description: A fast-paced overview of the entire platform for new administrators. Covers all major sections in under 3 minutes.

steps.value: Welcome to the Zorbit Quick Start! This overview covers all major platform sections.
steps.narration: Welcome to the Zorbit Quick Start. This overview covers all major platform sections.
Dashboard: your command center with configurable widgets.
Identity: manage users, organizations, and authentication providers.
Authorization: define roles and assign privileges to control access.
Audit: every action is logged with full traceability.
PII Vault: sensitive data is tokenized and stored securely.
Messaging: services communicate through Kafka events.
Products: business modules like the Product Configurator live here.
Settings: configure your organization's preferences and integrations.

steps.value: That's the quick tour! Explore each section in detail using the sidebar menu. Check out the other demo segments for deeper walkthroughs.
steps.narration: That's the quick tour. Explore each section in detail using the sidebar menu.
